title: SOAR Case Backlog Aging by Severity

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST CSF RS.MA-02 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF RS.MA-02 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.40: SOAR Case Backlog Aging by Severity.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.7.40' running on phantom:artifact, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=soar sourcetype=phantom:artifact status=open earliest=-90d
| eval age_days=(now()-_time)/86400
| where age_days>7
| stats values(name) as cases by severity
description: Lists open Splunk SOAR cases older than threshold by severity for incident management hygiene.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes soar and sourcetypes phantom:artifact. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for SOAR Case Backlog Aging by Severity. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Align severity with policy; (2) weekly war-room review; (3) auto-escalation rules. Indexes required: `index=soar`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=phantom:artifact`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk SOAR.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `phantom:artifact`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align severity with policy; (2) weekly war-room review; (3) auto-escalation rules.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=soar sourcetype=phantom:artifact status=open earliest=-90d
| eval age_days=(now()-_time)/86400
| where age_days>7
| stats values(name) as cases by severity
```

Understanding this SPL

**SOAR Case Backlog Aging by Severity (RS.MA-02)** — Lists open Splunk SOAR cases older than threshold by severity for incident management hygiene.

Documented **Data sources**: `phantom:artifact`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk SOAR. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: soar; **sourcetype**: phantom:artifact. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=soar, sourcetype=phantom:artifact, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where age_days>7` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by severity** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Bar chart
knownFalsePositives: Response time may extend during shift handoffs, holiday weekends, or major incident bursts.
